steps.title: Reduce the angle using periodicity
steps.content:
Cosine has period $2\pi$. To simplify $\cos\frac{565\pi}{6}$, I need to find a coterminal angle. Since $2\pi = \frac{12\pi}{6}$, I divide: $565 \div 12 = 47$ remainder \$1$. Therefore:
$$\frac{565\pi}{6} = \frac{(12 \cdot 47 + 1)\pi}{6} = 47 \cdot \frac{12\pi}{6} + \frac{\pi}{6} = 47(2\pi) + \frac{\pi}{6}$$
Since cosine has period $2\pi$, subtracting complete rotations doesn't change the value:
$$\cos\frac{565\pi}{6} = \cos\frac{\pi}{6}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the standard angle
steps.content: The angle $\frac{\pi}{6}$ radians equals 30°, which is a standard angle on the unit circle. Reading directly from the unit circle: $$\cos\frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
